title: If Prof. Jiang's Law of Proximity is right..Bitcoin MOONS
full_text: in May 2,024 a high school history teacher in Beijing told his students that America would go to war with Iran his students laughed nobody is laughing now there's a man teaching high school students in Beijing who has by any honest accounting one of the most extraordinary prediction records in modern geopolitical commentary he called Trump's 2,024 reelection months before the mainstream consensus acknowledged it was possible he predicted the escalation of US involvement with Iran in a classroom lecture in may 2,024 while his students took notes he predicted the logistical catastrophe that a Middle East escalation would create for American imperial overreach describing it with a game theoretic precision that career analysts at major think tanks failed to match his name is Jiang Xueqin he calls his method predictive history his YouTube channel has attracted more than 1.8 million subscribers the internet calls him China's Nostradamus serious critics call him something else entirely and we will get to that because intellectual honesty requires us to but here is the question this video is built around not whether Jong is right about everything he is not not whether he should be trusted uncritically he should not but a much more specific much more financially consequential question if Jiang's analysis of what he calls proximity the escalating civil war between old money financial elites and new money counter elites the inevitable bailout of parasitic economic bubbles with freshly printed fiat currency the structural collapse of petrodollar recycling through the Gulf if that analysis is directionally correct what happens to Bitcoin the answer when you follow the logic carefully and honestly is extraordinary and it is the subject of this video the man who called it before we can use Jiang's framework as an analytical lens we have to understand who he actually is and why that understanding matters for how we use his ideas Jiang Xueqin was born in Guangdong China in 1976 his family immigrated to Toronto Canada when he was six years old his father was a short order cook his mother was a seamstress he attended Yale University on scholarship graduating in 1999 with a degree in English literature he holds no graduate degree in economics political science history or any other field that would conventionally credential him as the kind of macro analyst he presents himself as this matters and we will come back to it after Yale Jiang returned to China first as a journalist then as an educator he spent years working inside China's most prestigious secondary schools Shenzhen Middle School Peking University High School Qinghua University High School he wrote a book about Chinese educational reform he became a researcher affiliated with the Harvard Graduate School of Education's Global Education Innovation Initiative around 2,022 he began recording his classroom lectures and uploading them to YouTube under the banner of his channel Predictive History the channel describes itself as dedicated to exploring whether what he calls psycho history the application of historical pattern recognition and game theory to predict geopolitical outcomes is actually possible the videos are distinctive Jiang stands at a whiteboard often in a classroom speaking with the measured cadence of a teacher rather than the urgent breathlessness of a pundit he draws diagrams he maps power structures he identifies what he sees as the hidden game theoretic logic beneath geopolitical events that mainstream analysis misses his episode called The Iran Trap released in 2,024 attracted international attention predicting the reelection of Donald Trump escalating US involvement in a conflict with Iran and eventual US loss in a prolonged conflict the first two of those predictions came true as of 2,026 so in an election delivered in May 2,024 he told his students directly the United States will go to war with Iran that lecture is now widely cited across the internet as one of the most specific correct predictions made by any public commentator in the past five years the record is real the accuracy on specific predictions is genuine enough to demand serious engagement but here is what intellectual honesty requires us to say in the same breath critics have raised pointed questions about the man behind the predictions he is not in any academic sense a professor the title that floats beneath his name across YouTube thumbnails and media write UPS is as one publication described it a fiction of accumulation a bachelor's degree in English literature dressed up by association with institutions impressive enough to imply more than they actually confer The Free Press described Jong as a conspiracy theorist who has promoted theories about the Illuminati Freemasons Jesuits and sabbateans through his YouTube channel critics have noted that his predictive framework relies on selective historical analogies speculative game theory reasoning and untestable assumptions his conclusions land with suspicious consistency US institutional collapse western elite civil war Chinese strategic patience vindicated Iranian resilience underestimated there is a through line and it runs in one direction a steady sophisticated drip of civilization pessimism aimed at western audiences we are presenting his framework because it is intellectually interesting because parts of it have proven directionally accurate and because the Bitcoin community has seized on his macro analysis to construct a compelling if speculative investment thesis we are not presenting it because we endorse him trust him uncritically or believe his more extreme claims that distinction is important keep it in mind throughout this video with that context established who Jong is what his record is and what his critics say let us now examine the specific analytical framework that the Bitcoin community has latched onto the framework is called proximity Jiang does not use the term the law of proximity as a formal precisely defined economic law it is not a peer reviewed academic concept what the Bitcoin community has labeled proximity is their synthesis of several interconnected ideas that run through Jung's geopolitical and economic analysis ideas about elite fragmentation internal American class conflict and the macro financial consequences that flow from that conflict understanding it requires understanding Jiang's broader model of how American imperial power actually works in his January 2,026 interview with the Greater Eurasia podcast Jiang explained his core framework after Nixon removed the dollar from the gold standard in 1971 the US needed two mechanisms to give the dollar value the first was the petrodollar the arrangement whereby Saudi Arabia would allow its oil to be purchased only in US dollars forcing every nation on earth to acquire dollars to participate in the global energy market the second mechanism was the recycling of petrodollars Gulf sovereign wealth funds flushed with oil revenue denominated in dollars needed somewhere to deploy that capital they deployed it into American financial assets Treasury bonds equities and increasingly into the venture capital ecosystem that financed the technology sector Jiang described the American economy as a Ponzi scheme reliant on Gulf Cooperation Council oil money flowing into artificial intelligence and technology companies this is the core of the system Gulf oil revenues flow into Silicon Valley Silicon Valley produces technology valuations those valuations generate paper wealth that sustains American consumer confidence that consumer confidence sustains demand for goods and that demand keeps the global trade system denominated in dollars which keeps the petrodollar arrangement intact which keeps the Gulf oil revenues flowing back into the system it is as Jiang describes it circular self reinforcing and deeply fragile now here is where proximity enters the picture within the system there are two distinct power centers two elites and they are not the same elite even though from the outside they can look like a unified ruling class the first is what we might call the old financial elite Wall Street the Federal Reserve Network Treasury the traditional institutions of American financial power the ones that profit from bond markets from dollar hegemony from the existing financial architecture these are the people who benefit most directly from the petrodollar recycling system exactly as it currently exists their wealth is denominated in and dependent on the continued credibility of dollar denominated financial assets the second is what we might call the counter elite Silicon Valley the artificial intelligence investment complex venture capital the new billionaire class that built its fortune on technology valuations rather than financial instruments Elon Musk's world Peter Thiel's world the world of AI infrastructure investment of data center construction of the technology sector that consumed three quarters of a trillion dollars in capital investment in 2,025 alone these two elites have different interests different constituencies different ideological orientations and increasingly in Jiang's analysis different and conflicting relationships with the existing financial architecture Jiang's predictive framework traces American collapse not to recent political events but through a distinct decades long historical and macroeconomic lineage an empire managed by what he describes as insecure transgressive elites who have been conditioned to view reality as a zero sum competition will inevitably overextend itself it will rely on the alchemy of fiat currency and the blunt force of military power to sustain the fragile illusion of control now here is the specific chain of logic that makes the Bitcoin community's ears perk up the counter elite Silicon Valley artificial intelligence venture capital has built an investment ecosystem that Jong and others argue has characteristics of a bubble the AI investment thesis requires enormous capital deployment data centers chips infrastructure talent the Gulf sovereign wealth funds are major investors in this ecosystem their petrodollars recycled through the system Jong describes are a significant source of the capital that has inflated AI valuations from the bursting of the AI investment bubble to the systemic weaknesses threatening to push the American empire into a state of micromilitarism and domestic instability Jiang warns these global maneuvers are the setup for high stakes confrontation with Iran to cement American hegemony The Iran war the one Jiang predicted has now disrupted the Gulf energy infrastructure that underpins the petrodollar recycling mechanism the strikes on South Pars the retaliatory attacks on Ross Laufen the destabilization of Gulf energy economies these are not just military events they are financial events they threaten the circular flow of capital that keeps the American financial system as Jiang calls it operational when that circular flow is disrupted when Gulf sovereign wealth funds face losses on their energy infrastructure when oil price spikes create inflation that the Fed cannot accommodate with rate cuts when the AI bubble faces a confidence crisis because the petrodollar recycling that funded it is threatened both elites face an existential financial moment simultaneously the old financial elite faces a bond market crisis if dollar credibility erodes the counter elite faces a valuation collapse if the AI bubble deflates without new capital to sustain it and in that moment in the moment where both elites are simultaneously threatened Jiang's framework predicts something very specific about what happens next they demand a bailout both of them from the government funded by the one mechanism that the government has unlimited access to money printing quantitative easing fiat currency creation dollar debasement proximity in the Bitcoin community's framing of Jiang's ideas is precisely this the moment when two competing elites whose bubbles are collapsing simultaneously converge on the Federal Reserve and demand that it print money to save them both the proximity of their respective crises forces a monetary response that debases the currency at a scale that dwarfs anything seen since 2,008 and in that environment in that specific macro scenario Bitcoin's hard cap of 21 million coins becomes not just theoretically interesting but operationally critical to understand why the Iran war specifically rather than any generic geopolitical crisis is the trigger event that Jiang's framework identifies for the proximity scenario you have to understand the role of the Gulf Cooperation Council in the global financial architecture the GCC Saudi Arabia the UAE Qatar Kuwait Bahrain and Oman is not merely an oil producing region it is the financial circulatory system of the dollar denominated global order through the petrodollar mechanism every barrel of oil sold generates dollar denominated revenue that flows back into American financial assets through their sovereign wealth funds Gulf states have become among the largest institutional investors in American technology American real estate American private equity and American government debt The Abu Dhabi Investment Authority the Kuwait Investment Authority the Public Investment Fund of Saudi Arabia the Qatar Investment Authority these are not minor players they collectively manage trillions of dollars in assets and their allocation decisions move markets Chung's analysis identified a specific risk if the Iran conflict destabilizes GCC economies if the strikes on South Pars and Rossloven damage Qatar's LNG export capacity if the oil price spike creates secondary economic stress across the Gulf these sovereign wealth funds face a crisis of their own their portfolios are marked down their domestic fiscal positions are stressed their political leaders face domestic pressure and their appetite for continuing to recycle petrodollars into American financial assets the very mechanism that sustains the US Ponzi scheme is reduced this matters for artificial intelligence specifically because Gulf sovereign wealth funds have been among the most aggressive allocators into the AI investment ecosystem The Public Investment Fund of Saudi Arabia made enormous bets on technology infrastructure Abu Dhabi's Mubadala Fund has been a significant investor in AI ventures removing their capital from this ecosystem does not merely reduce the pace of investment it threatens the valuations of assets that the counter elite built its political and financial power upon Jong's framing about Dubai being a mirage sustained by capital inflows that a single escalation could collapse this illusion and trigger a flight of billions in capital is not merely geopolitical theater it describes a financial domino chain that has direct implications for where that fleeing capital goes here's the question that the Bitcoin community has been asking since the Iran war began when capital flees the Gulf when petrodollars stop recycling efficiently into American financial assets when sovereign wealth funds begin reducing their exposure to a system that suddenly looks much less stable than it did six months ago where does that capital go some of it goes to gold we saw that in the initial price move but gold's performance in this specific macro environment Fed hawkish strong dollar oil driven inflation has been poor gold fell over 6% silver fell over 13% some of it goes to US Treasuries but if the dollar itself is being questioned if the weaponization of dollar sanctions and the seizure of foreign assets has eroded global trust in dollar denominated instruments as Jiang's analysis suggests then the reflexive flight to Treasuries is weakened the weaponization of the US dollar through sanctions and the seizure of foreign assets is seen in Jong's framework as a rotting global trust prompting moves toward de dollarization and some of it a fraction but potentially a historically significant fraction flows into bitcoin not because bitcoin is a perfect safe haven not because it is immune to macro forces we have already established clearly that Bitcoin fell into the 69 to 70 thousand dollar range on March 19th an approximately 4 to 5% decline driven by risk off sentiment as the Fed spoke and oil surged but because in a world where the traditional stores of value are failing where dollar credibility is being questioned where the recycling mechanism that sustained the global financial order is under direct military threat the asset that is independent of all of these systems that cannot be sanctioned that cannot be inflated that has a mathematically fixed supply begins to look categorically different from everything else one of Jiang's most viral intellectual moments captured in an Instagram reel from March 2,026 that accumulated millions of views was a deceptively simple question he posed to his audience if money can be created infinitely if the Federal Reserve can in principle conjure trillions of dollars into existence with a keystroke then why does poverty exist why are there people who cannot afford food housing healthcare if the solution to every financial problem is simply to print more money why is that solution not applied universally Jiang's answer is not complex but it is important because money is not wealth money is a claim on wealth and wealth real wealth the actual goods and services and infrastructure and human capacity that makes life better is limited you cannot print a barrel of oil you cannot print a semiconductor you cannot print a trained nurse or a functional power grid or an acre of arable farmland when you print money without a corresponding increase in real productive capacity you are not creating wealth you are diluting the claim that every existing dollar has on the existing pool of real wealth you are redistributing from holders of cash and cash equivalent assets which disproportionately includes ordinary people with savings accounts and pension funds to holders of real assets and hard money which disproportionately includes the already wealthy inflation is in Jiang's framing a form of tax invisible unlegislated massively regressive this argument is not original to Jang it is the foundation of Austrian economics of sound money advocacy of the intellectual tradition that stretches from Mises through Hayek to Friedman but Jiang's framing makes it accessible and urgent in a way that reaches audiences who have never encountered these ideas in their original academic form and for bitcoin the argument is devastating in its simplicity bitcoin has a hard cap of 21 million coins not a soft cap that can be renegotiated not a target that can be missed not a commitment that can be walked back by a committee of 12 people in Washington a mathematically enforced cryptographically secured absolute limit no government can vote to print more bitcoin no central bank can issue more bitcoin to bail out a failing bank no sovereign wealth fund can create new bitcoin to stabilize a currency peg when Jong says the US economy is a Ponzi scheme that relies on Gulf oil money flowing into tech startups and that if those Gulf oil revenues are disrupted the scheme faces its moment of maximum stress he is describing the exact macro environment in which the hard supply cap of Bitcoin becomes most relevant the Ponzi scheme needs new money to survive bitcoin cannot be new money it can only ever be the same 21 million coins increasingly recognized increasingly demanded not increasing in supply now this is where we need to be extremely careful about the distinction between Jiang's actual claims and the Bitcoin community's interpretation of those claims Jung does not say that bitcoin will go parabolic he is in most documented contexts skeptical of cryptocurrency as an investment vehicle his analysis focuses on geopolitics power structures and institutional decline the Bitcoin goes parabolic conclusion is not his it is the Bitcoin community's inference from his macro framework that distinction matters but it does not make the inference wrong the inference is this if Jong's macro framework is directionally correct if the petrodollar recycling mechanism is being disrupted if the competing elites face simultaneous financial crises if the government's response is inevitably massive monetary expansion then the asset that cannot be expanded gains the most from that expansion that asset is bitcoin the inference may be wrong the proximity scenario may not materialize the Fed may find a way to navigate the crisis without massive debasement the Gulf economies may stabilize faster than Yang's model predicts but the logic of the inference is sound if the premises hold the conclusion follows there is a dimension of Jung's analysis that goes beyond economics and into something that makes many mainstream commentators deeply uncomfortable and it is the dimension that the Bitcoin community finds perhaps most compelling as a driver of the debasement scenario Jiang's argument articulated in multiple videos and captured in the community analysis that circulated widely in March 2,026 is that the Iran war is not primarily a military or geopolitical event in the traditional sense it is not in his framework about nuclear proliferation or regional stability or Israeli security or any of the stated rationales it is in his analysis about domestic American politics specifically about the use of emergency powers to manage an increasingly ungovernable political and economic situation at home according to Jiang's framework the current trajectory of US foreign policy is accelerating a pattern of imperial over stretch that risks geopolitical blowback domestically his analysis posits that elite fragmentation and internal conflict mirror historical patterns of imperial decline the argument goes like this when a society faces the kind of simultaneous internal stresses that the United States faces in 2,026 a national debt that has crossed 39 trillion dollars and is approaching 40 trillion an AI bubble of uncertain sustainability political polarization at historic levels institutional trust at historic lows political leaders throughout history have reached for external conflict as a mechanism for domestic consolidation war in this analysis is not merely a foreign policy tool it is a political technology it concentrates executive power it justifies emergency spending that bypasses normal legislative constraints it creates a rally around the flag dynamic that suppresses internal criticism it allows the executive to invoke war powers that dramatically expand governmental authority over the economy and critically for the bitcoin thesis it provides political cover for the kind of monetary expansion that would otherwise face massive political resistance you cannot easily justify printing $5 trillion in peacetime people notice people ask questions people vote differently but in wartime when the nation is under existential threat when troops are deployed when energy infrastructure is being struck by missiles the political calculus changes emergency spending becomes patriotic deficit expansion becomes necessary and the Federal Reserve nominally independent but practically constrained by political reality finds itself in an environment where resisting monetary expansion is not merely economically contested but politically untenable in Jiang's framework an empire that relies on the alchemy of fiat currency and the blunt violence of the military industrial complex to sustain the fragile illusion of control eventually collapses under the weight of its own contradictions the collapse in the Bitcoin community's application of this framework does not look like an explosion it looks like a slow motion debasement a gradual politically justified crisis by crisis expansion of the money supply that happens in increments small enough to be politically manageable but large enough cumulatively to represent a fundamental shift in the purchasing power of the dollar each increment of debasement is a win for Bitcoin not because Bitcoin is a trading vehicle but because Bitcoin is the hardest asset that exists and every dollar of debasement makes hard assets more attractive relative to the debased currency stop right here for a second because if you have made it this far into this video you have just sat through a deep dive into game theory petrodollar recycling elite power structures and monetary philosophy that is not what most people do with their Friday night or their Tuesday morning or whatever time it is where you are right now and that right there is the point I wanna know where in the world you're watching this from right now drop it in the comments your city the exact time and what the weather looks like outside your window because this channel reaches people from SAO Paulo to Seoul from Lagos to London from Riyadh to Rio and I want to see the map of people who cared enough about understanding how the world actually works to make it halfway through a 40 minute analysis of Jiang Xueqin and Bitcoin debasement theory city time weather one line that is all I'm asking and if you have not subscribed yet do it right now before we keep going because what comes next is the part most channels will not touch the honest critique of Jong's framework and then the specific price and macro scenarios for Bitcoin if the proximity thesis proves correct you do not want to miss that let's keep going this video would be intellectually dishonest if it presented Jiang's framework as a settled reliable predictive model and simply drew a straight line from his analysis to a Bitcoin price target the honest version of this analysis requires us to examine where Jiang's framework might be wrong and what those potential errors mean for the Bitcoin thesis built upon it challenge 1 the prediction record is selective critics noted that Jang's predictions rely on selective historical analogies speculative game theory reasoning and untestable assumptions the predictions that came true Trump's re election Iran escalation were made in an environment where the base rates for those outcomes were already reasonably high calling Trump's re election in May 2,024 was impressive relative to mainstream media but it was consistent with what many serious political forecasters already saw in the polling data the Iran escalation prediction was directionally correct but the specific mechanism and timing were less precise than the viral summary suggest predictive history as a methodology has a well documented problem the human mind is extraordinarily skilled at finding patterns in historical data an extraordinarily poor at distinguishing between genuine signal and posthoc rationalization the same intellectual toolkit that generates insightful predictions can generate dozens of confident but wrong ones that simply do not go viral because they failed challenge 2 the conspiracy theory adjacency problem The Free Press described Jong as a conspiracy theorist who has promoted theories about the Illuminati Freemasons Jesuits and sabbateans through his YouTube channel this is not a peripheral criticism when a commentator mixes genuine geopolitical insight with demonstrably unfalsifiable conspiracy frameworks it creates a serious epistemological problem for the audience how do you separate the sound analysis from the noise when both are delivered with the same confident pedagogical authority the answer is carefully very carefully by insisting on primary sources by checking claims independently by refusing to accept the package deal of accepting everything or nothing challenge 3 the China Alignment question the question nobody in his comment section is eager to ask how does a man operating in Beijing produce content that maps with uncanny precision onto Chinese Communist Party strategic messaging for an audience of millions of Westerners this is not a trivial question every analytical framework including Jiang's is shaped by the interests and constraints of its producer if JoJianghn's analytical conclusions consistently serve Chinese strategic communication goals that consistency is worth noting it does not automatically make his analysis wrong but it is a reason for skepticism that is proportional to the consistency of the alignment challenge 4 the debasement timeline problem even if Jiang's macro framework is directionally correct even if the petrodollar recycling mechanism is genuinely under threat even if competing elites are genuinely moving toward a proximity style confrontation the timeline for these dynamics to manifest in ways that force a monetary response is deeply uncertain the US dollar status as the global reserve currency has proven more durable than analysts predicting its collapse have consistently expected the petrodollar mechanism has survived oil shocks sanctions regimes the rise of the euro the rise of Chinese yuan alternatives and the explicit stated desire of bricks nations to de dollarize it is being stressed it may eventually fail in a meaningful way but eventually and now are very different time frames for an investment thesis so having laid out the challenges to Jong's framework honestly what do we do with the Bitcoin thesis the answer is uh we hold it with the appropriate degree of confidence not certainty not dismissal a calibrated assessment that acknowledges the directional logic while respecting the genuine uncertainty about whether when and how severely the macro conditions materialize the Bitcoin thesis built on Jong's framework does not require Jong to be right about everything it requires the following much more modest set of conditions to hold 1 the US federal debt approaching $40 trillion represents a genuine long term constraint on fiscal and monetary policy 2 that energy price shocks driven by Middle East conflict create genuine inflationary pressure that the Fed cannot easily accommodate 3 that some fraction of the capital that flows out of stressed Gulf sovereign wealth funds and deflating technology bubbles is looking for assets that are independent of the dollar denominated financial system four that bitcoin with its 21 million hard cap and its institutional adoption infrastructure of spot etfs and corporate treasury allocations is capable of absorbing meaningful flows of that capital none of these four conditions require Jong to be China's Nostradamus none of them require his conspiracy frameworks to be true all four of them are conditions that can be evaluated independently against publicly available data and all four of them as of March 2,026 appear to be at least directionally accurate look before we continue let me make this clear obviously this video is not financial advice I'm sharing my vision and strategy but you are the CEO of your own capital never risk money you cannot afford to lose as the markets are engineered to be volatile do your own research keep your emotions in check and only play the games you are prepared to win now let's get into the data let us now build the specific mechanism step by step through which the proximity scenario if it materializes would translate into extraordinary Bitcoin price performance this is not speculation about price targets this is a causal chain follow each link and evaluate it independently link 1 the trigger event Gulf capital flight the Iran war has struck South Pars the world's largest gas field it has threatened Ras Laffan Qatar's primary LNG export terminal the Gulf energy infrastructure that underpins petrodollar recycling is under direct military threat for the first time in a generation Gulf sovereign wealth funds managing collectively trillions of dollars in assets are reassessing their risk exposure some fraction of that capital is being repositioned if Jong's analysis holds and GCC economic instability follows those petrodollars being repositioned accelerate inflows to alternative stores of value including Bitcoin etfs this is not hypothetical ETF flow data from the first phase of the Iran conflict showed institutional inflows into bitcoin vehicles even as spot price fell suggesting that the institutional buyer base was using the dip to accumulate not to exit link 2 the AI bubble stress counter elite pressure for QE the AI investment ecosystem has consumed extraordinary amounts of capital the valuations implied by that capital deployment require continued growth in revenue and user adoption at rates that are increasingly difficult to sustain as Gulf sovereign wealth fund realocation reduces the marginal flow of capital into technology as interest rates remain high making the opportunity cost of holding zero yield technology positions more expensive and as AI revenue reality becomes clearer relative to the AI investment narrative the conditions for a significant technology valuation correction are present when that correction comes when the counter elite's financial position is threatened the political pressure for monetary relief will be intense the counter elite is not politically passive their demand for financial relief whether through explicit QE through regulatory changes that inflate asset prices or through other mechanisms will be politically amplified link 3 the Old elites bond market problem convergence simultaneously if the dollar's reserve currency status erodes even modestly the old financial elite faces its own existential threat the Treasury market the foundation of the dollar denominated financial system requires ongoing demand from foreign central banks and sovereign wealth funds if that demand diminishes Treasury yields rise if Treasury yields rise materially the interest payments on nearly $40 trillion in federal debt become economically catastrophic The Federal Reserve faces pressure to intervene to buy Treasuries to suppress yields to monetize federal debt this is quantitative easing by another name at another moment in another political context but functionally the same mechanism that Bitcoin has been designed to be immune to link 4 the Fed capitulation from hawkish to emergency the Federal Reserve held rates on March 18th explicitly citing energy prices as an inflation risk this is the hawkish moment but Jong's framework and basic macro logic predicts that this hawkish moment has a ceiling when the financial system faces enough simultaneous stress from technology valuation collapse from Treasury market dysfunction from banking sector pressure on commercial real estate the Fed's mandate for financial stability overrides its mandate for price stability we saw this in 2,023 with the Silicon Valley bank crisis the Fed was hiking rates until the moment a significant bank failure forced a temporary liquidity intervention scale that dynamic up by an order of magnitude and you have the proximity scenario the Fed forced to pivot from hawkish to emergency accommodation not because inflation is solved but because the alternative is systemic financial collapse link five the debasement math Bitcoin's hardness in context if the Federal Reserve expands its balance sheet by 3 to five trillion dollars the scale of intervention that the proximity scenario would require what happens to the purchasing power of the dollar relative to hard assets history provides the answer Between 2008 and 2,022 the Federal Reserve expanded its balance sheet from approximately $900 billion to approximately $9 trillion a tenfold increase over that same period gold approximately quadrupled from roughly $800 to roughly $3,000 bitcoin from its 2013 institutional visibility through 2,021 went from under $1,000 to $69,000 the relationship is not mechanically precise monetary expansion does not translate to asset price appreciation in a linear or predictable way but the directional relationship is clear and consistent when the money supply expands dramatically hard assets with fixed or constrained supply appreciate dramatically relative to the expanding currency bitcoin supply is not just constrained it is absolutely fixed 21 million coins forever no committee can change this no war can print more no bailout can dilute the existing supply in the proximity scenario if the debasement is as dramatic as the scale of the crisis implies Bitcoin's response could be correspondingly dramatic hence Bitcoin goes parabolic not because Jong endorsed this conclusion he did not but because the logical chain from his macro premises to this financial conclusion is coherent traceable and grounded in the same monetary mechanics that have governed hard asset behavior for the entire history of fiat currency let us now anchor this analytical framework to the current on chain and market data because the thesis only matters if the setup is actually present as of March 19th 2,026 the Bitcoin market structure reflects a specific combination of characteristics worth examining carefully first long term holder behavior blockchain analytics show that wallets holding bitcoin for 12 months or more the conviction holder base that institutional adoption is built did not sell in any significant quantity during the March 19th price decline Over $500 million in leveraged long positions were liquidated but that pain was concentrated in the derivatives market among speculators using leverage of five 10 or 20 times the spot holder base the ETF investors the corporate treasury allocations the conviction buyers held this matters because it tells you the composition of the remaining holder base after the liquidation what is left after the weaker hands are shaken out is a more concentrated more conviction driven more institutionally anchored holder base the people holding bitcoin tonight are not holding it because it is going up and momentum is with them they are holding it because their investment thesis whether it is the having cycle the debasement hedge the proximity scenario or some combination remains intact despite the price action second derivatives positioning funding rates flipped slightly negative after the March 19th liquidation event this means the market structure on the derivative side has shifted from being overloaded with leveraged longs the crowded position that made Bitcoin vulnerable to the cascade that played out to a more balanced or even modestly short leaning configuration historically this type of derivatives reset has preceded significant price recoveries in bitcoin third the having cycle the most recent Bitcoin having occurred in April 2,024 the historical pattern across three previous having cycles is that the 12 to 18 months following a having represent Bitcoin's strongest sustained bull market performance we are still within that window macro turbulence of the Iran war and the Fed's hawkish stance do not change the supply side mathematics of the having they complicate the demand side picture in the short term but as macro conditions eventually shift as they always do the supply constraint becomes a more powerful driver fourth the institutional infrastructure the spot Bitcoin ETF ecosystem that now exists in the United States represents a permanently lowered barrier to institutional bitcoin allocation when the next risk off cycle turns to risk on when the macro environment shifts from hawkish Fed and strong dollar to accommodative Fed and weakening dollar the ETF infrastructure means that institutional capital can move into Bitcoin at a speed and scale that was not possible in previous cycles the setup as of today looks like this a cleaned up derivatives market an institutionally anchored long term holder base a having cycle that historically argues for continued price appreciation and an ETF infrastructure that makes the next institutional wave structurally easier than any previous one on top of which sits Jong's macro framework which argues that the debasement pressure that drives capital into hard assets is not receding but accelerating this is what the parabolic thesis is built on not hype not optimism a framework a mechanism a dataset and a catalyst let me now give you two clear scenarios the proximity scenario and the alternative so that you can form your own view rather than simply accepting the bullish framing scenario 1 the proximity scenario materializes in this scenario over the next 12 to 18 months the following sequence plays out the Iran conflict extends long enough to sustain energy market disruption Brent crude remains above $100 European gas prices remain elevated the inflationary impulse is sustained not brief the AI investment ecosystem faces a significant evaluation correction as Gulf capital repatriation reduces inflows as high interest rates make growth at any cost investing untenable and as AI revenue reality falls short of AI investment narrative the technology sector correction is significant 20 to 30% on the NASDAQ from current levels The Federal Reserve facing simultaneous pressure from equity market stress Treasury market dysfunction and banking sector weakness on commercial real estate pivots from its current hawkish stance to emergency accommodation whether that accommodation is labeled quantitative easing balance sheet expansion yield curve control or some other euphemism the functional result is dollar debasement at scale Gulf sovereign wealth funds reassessing their exposure to dollar denominated assets in light of geopolitical risk and reduced oil revenues reallocate some fraction of their capital toward assets that are independent of the dollar system bitcoin etfs capture a meaningful share of that reallocation in this scenario which requires Jiang's directional analysis to be correct on multiple counts simultaneously the macro setup for Bitcoin is the most favorable in the asset's history you have institutional infrastructure having cycled tailwinds a cleaned up derivatives market and a debasement catalyst of historic proportions all converging in this scenario the question for Bitcoin is not whether it appreciates significantly it is what the ceiling looks like scenario 2 the proximity scenario does not materialize or materializes much later in this scenario the Iran conflict deescalates faster than expected a diplomatic framework reduces the direct threat to Gulf energy infrastructure oil prices fall back toward 90 to 95 dollars over three to six months the inflationary impulse recedes The Federal Reserve's hawkish stance is vindicated inflation falls back toward target without requiring a dramatic economic crisis the one rate cut projected for 2,026 happens in the second half of the year and is positioned as a normalized easing not an emergency pivot the AI investment ecosystem stabilizes or even reinflates as new applications demonstrate revenue potential and as interest rate expectations moderate Gulf sovereign wealth funds do not significantly repatriate capital because their energy revenues recovering with oil prices remain adequate to sustain their investment programs in this scenario Jong's macro framework fails to materialize on the timeline that the Bitcoin thesis depends on bitcoin remains subject to the same cross asset correlations that governed it in 2,022 moving roughly with risk assets affected by dollar strength constrained by the absence of the massive debasement catalyst in this scenario Bitcoin's performance is driven by its own supply dynamics the having cycle the institutional adoption arc the ETF infrastructure rather than by the macro debasement thesis solid performance relative to other assets but not the parabolic move that the proximity scenario implies which scenario do I think is more likely I do not know and I want to be very clear that neither does anyone else including Professor Yang his framework is not a crystal ball it is an analytical lens that has proven directionally useful in some contexts and directionally wrong in others what I do know is that the question matters the difference between these two scenarios is not a small difference in returns it is the difference between Bitcoin as a solid portfolio diversifier in a macro recovery and Bitcoin as the defining asset of a decade defining monetary debasement event understanding the mechanism understanding why the proximity scenario would produce the specific outcome it would produce for Bitcoin is not optional for any serious allocator whether you believe Jong or not whether you invest in
